zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh Malet於大約1390年出生在英國薩默塞特時，他的父親Baldwin Malet43歲，母親Amicia Lyffe32歲。他於大約1414年與Joan Roynon在英國薩默塞特布利其沃特結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和5個女兒的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Malet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: nickname from a diminutive of Mal ‘bad’ or from a derivative of the related Old French word maleit ‘accursed’ (see Mallet 1). Compare Malette .
French: from the Old French personal name Malet, a pet form of Malo (compare Mallet 2).

variants.name: Mal
meanings: Slovenian: nickname for a physically small man, from mal ‘small, little’.

variants.name: Malette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gaston, Alphonse, Francois, Jacques, Rejean, Serge.
French: variant or feminine form of Malet and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this, reflecting the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh Malet was born about 1390, in Somerset, England, his father, Baldwin Malet, was 43 and his mother, Amicia Lyffe, was 32. He married Joan Roynon about 1414, in Bridgwater, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 5 daughters.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh Maletが約1390年にイギリスソメルセトで生まれた時，父Baldwin Maletは43歳，母Amicia Lyffeは32歳であった。約1414年にイギリスソメルセトブリドグワターでJoan Roynonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh Malet(이)가 대략 1390년 영국 서머세트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Baldwin Malet(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Amicia Lyffe(은)는 32세였다. 그는 대략 1414년 영국 서머세트 브리지왓어에서 Joan Roynon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다.
